Functions for initializing, deinitializing, shrinking, pausing, and growing a loading layer animation.

# Purpose
The code defines a set of functions to manage a `LoadingLayer` structure, which is likely part of a graphical user interface. The `LoadingLayer` is initialized and deinitialized using the [`loading_layer_init`](<#loading_layer_init>) and [`loading_layer_deinit`](<#loading_layer_deinit>) functions, respectively. These functions set up and tear down the `LoadingLayer`, including its associated `ProgressLayer`. The [`loading_layer_init`](<#loading_layer_init>) function initializes the `LoadingLayer` with a specified frame and sets a corner radius for the `ProgressLayer`. The [`loading_layer_deinit`](<#loading_layer_deinit>) function pauses any ongoing animations and deinitializes the `ProgressLayer`.

The code also provides functions to animate the `LoadingLayer`. The [`loading_layer_shrink`](<#loading_layer_shrink>) function animates the shrinking of the `LoadingLayer` over a specified duration and delay, using a property animation to transition the layer's frame. The [`loading_layer_grow`](<#loading_layer_grow>) function performs the opposite operation, animating the growth of the `LoadingLayer`. Both functions use the [`loading_layer_pause`](<#loading_layer_pause>) function to stop any ongoing animations before starting a new one. The animations are configured with an ease-out curve and can have custom handlers for when the animation stops. The code uses the `property_animation_create_layer_frame` function to create animations and manages them with the `animation_schedule` and `animation_unschedule` functions.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `loading_layer.h`
- `applib/graphics/gtypes.h`
- `applib/ui/property_animation.h`
- `string.h`

### loading\_layer\_init<!-- {{#callable:loading_layer_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/voice/loading_layer.c#L24>)

Initializes a `LoadingLayer` structure and its associated `ProgressLayer` with a specified frame.
- **Inputs**:
    - `loading_layer`: A pointer to a `LoadingLayer` structure to initialize.
    - `frame`: A pointer to a `GRect` structure that defines the frame for the `LoadingLayer`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Assigns the `full_frame` member of `loading_layer` to the value pointed to by `frame`.
    - Initializes the `progress_layer` member of `loading_layer` using [`progress_layer_init`](<../ui/progress_layer.c.md#progress_layer_init>) with the same `frame`.
    - Sets the corner radius of `progress_layer` to `PROGRESS_SUGGESTED_CORNER_RADIUS` using [`progress_layer_set_corner_radius`](<../ui/progress_layer.c.md#progress_layer_set_corner_radius>).
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the `loading_layer` structure in place.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`progress_layer_init`](<../ui/progress_layer.c.md#progress_layer_init>)
    - [`progress_layer_set_corner_radius`](<../ui/progress_layer.c.md#progress_layer_set_corner_radius>)


---
### loading\_layer\_deinit<!-- {{#callable:loading_layer_deinit}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/voice/loading_layer.c#L34>)

Deinitializes a `LoadingLayer` by pausing any ongoing animations and deinitializing its `ProgressLayer`.
- **Inputs**:
    - `loading_layer`: A pointer to a `LoadingLayer` structure that needs to be deinitialized.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Call [`loading_layer_pause`](<#loading_layer_pause>) to pause any ongoing animations associated with the `loading_layer`.
    - Call [`progress_layer_deinit`](<../ui/progress_layer.c.md#progress_layer_deinit>) to deinitialize the `progress_layer` member of the `loading_layer`.
- **Output**: No return value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`loading_layer_pause`](<#loading_layer_pause>)
    - [`progress_layer_deinit`](<../ui/progress_layer.c.md#progress_layer_deinit>)


---
### loading\_layer\_shrink<!-- {{#callable:loading_layer_shrink}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/voice/loading_layer.c#L39>)

Animates the shrinking of a `LoadingLayer` by reducing its width to zero over a specified duration and delay.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``loading_layer``: A pointer to the `LoadingLayer` structure that will be animated.
    - ``delay``: The delay in milliseconds before the animation starts.
    - ``duration``: The duration in milliseconds over which the animation occurs.
    - ``stopped_handler``: A callback function that is called when the animation stops.
    - ``context``: A pointer to user-defined data passed to the `stopped_handler`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Pauses any ongoing animation on the `loading_layer` by calling [`loading_layer_pause`](<#loading_layer_pause>).
    - Sets the frame of the `loading_layer` to its full size using [`layer_set_frame`](<../ui/layer.c.md#layer_set_frame>).
    - Initializes `start` and `stop` `GRect` structures to represent the starting and ending frames of the animation, with `stop` having a width of zero.
    - Creates a `PropertyAnimation` to animate the `loading_layer` from `start` to `stop`.
    - Checks if the `PropertyAnimation` creation was successful; if not, exits the function.
    - Retrieves the `Animation` object from the `PropertyAnimation`.
    - Sets the delay, duration, and easing curve for the animation using `animation_set_delay`, `animation_set_duration`, and `animation_set_curve`.
    - Sets the animation handlers, including the `stopped_handler`, using `animation_set_handlers`.
    - Stores the `Animation` object in the `loading_layer` structure.
    - Schedules the animation to start using `animation_schedule`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the `loading_layer` and schedules an animation.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`loading_layer_pause`](<#loading_layer_pause>)
    - [`layer_set_frame`](<../ui/layer.c.md#layer_set_frame>)
    - [`property_animation_get_animation`](<../ui/property_animation.c.md#property_animation_get_animation>)


---
### loading\_layer\_pause<!-- {{#callable:loading_layer_pause}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/voice/loading_layer.c#L69>)

Pauses the animation of a `LoadingLayer` if it is currently scheduled.
- **Inputs**:
    - `loading_layer`: A pointer to a `LoadingLayer` structure whose animation is to be paused.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if the animation associated with `loading_layer` is scheduled using `animation_is_scheduled`.
    - If the animation is scheduled, call `animation_unschedule` to pause it.
- **Output**: No output is returned.


---
### loading\_layer\_grow<!-- {{#callable:loading_layer_grow}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/voice/loading_layer.c#L75>)

Animates the growth of a `LoadingLayer` from zero width to its full width over a specified duration and delay.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``loading_layer``: A pointer to a `LoadingLayer` structure that will be animated.
    - ``delay``: The delay in milliseconds before the animation starts.
    - ``duration``: The duration in milliseconds over which the animation occurs.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls [`loading_layer_pause`](<#loading_layer_pause>) to stop any ongoing animation on the `loading_layer`.
    - Checks if `duration` is zero; if true, sets the `loading_layer` frame to `full_frame` and exits.
    - Initializes a `GRect` named `start` with the `full_frame` of `loading_layer` but sets its width to zero.
    - Sets the frame of `loading_layer` to `start`.
    - Creates a `PropertyAnimation` from `start` to `full_frame`; if creation fails, exits the function.
    - Retrieves the `Animation` from the `PropertyAnimation`.
    - Sets the delay, duration, and curve (ease out) for the animation.
    - Assigns the animation to `loading_layer->animation`.
    - Schedules the animation to start.
- **Output**: None (the function modifies the `loading_layer` in place).
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`loading_layer_pause`](<#loading_layer_pause>)
    - [`layer_set_frame`](<../ui/layer.c.md#layer_set_frame>)
    - [`property_animation_get_animation`](<../ui/property_animation.c.md#property_animation_get_animation>)
